page: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highcpu-4 is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance based on the x86_64 architecture. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical AMD EPYC 9B14 cores with multithreading enabled. The instance is configured with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB per vCPU. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance scoring 2864.18 and Geekbench text processing scoring 2355.0, both placing in the top 25%. However, multi-core capabilities are weaker, with stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core scores falling into the bottom 25%. Memory subsystem performance is a notable bottleneck; the Passmark memory score is in the bottom 10%, and uncached memory write bandwidth is limited to 15.4 GB/sec.

This hardware profile is suited for single-threaded tasks, text processing, and lightweight utility workloads that do not demand high memory throughput or large memory footprints. It is less suitable for memory-intensive databases, multi-threaded software compilation, or medium-to-large language model inference, where benchmarks show poor to weak performance. The dedicated AMD EPYC cores provide predictable compute allocation, making it a cost-efficient option for compute-bound tasks requiring low memory overhead.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highcpu-4 is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance powered by an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor. It provides 4 dedicated vCPUs across 2 physical cores, paired with 8.0 GB of RAM, resulting in a 2.0 GB per vCPU ratio. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks reveal a contrast between strong single-core execution, such as stress-ng single-core and Geekbench text processing, and weaker multi-core and memory subsystem throughput. Specifically, memory bandwidth and multi-threaded decompression benchmarks fall into the lower tiers, with Passmark memory scores in the bottom 10%. Consequently, this instance is best suited for single-threaded applications, basic text processing, and lightweight compute tasks with minimal memory requirements, rather than memory-heavy databases or parallelized compilation workloads.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highcpu-4 is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs and 8 GB of RAM, delivering strong single-core performance alongside limited memory bandwidth.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highcpu-4 is an x86_64 instance with 4 AMD EPYC vCPUs and 8 GB RAM, optimized for single-threaded compute workloads.
tagline: A compute-focused virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC cores and a low memory-to-CPU ratio for single-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by AMD EPYC 9B14 processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs and 2 physical cores.
Configured with 8.0 GB of RAM, providing 2.0 GB of memory per vCPU.
Strong single-core CPU performance and text processing capabilities.
Lacks local storage and GPU acceleration.
Constrained memory bandwidth and multi-core scaling performance.
Best suited for single-threaded tasks and lightweight compute utilities.
